description.en: The address (IPv4Address) without network information. >>> interface = IPv4Interface('192.0.2.5/24') >>> interface.ip IPv4Address('192.0.2.5')

info.excerpt: The address (IPv4Address) without network information. >>> interface = IPv4Interface('192.0.2.5/24') >>> interface.ip IPv4Address('192.0.2.5')

description.en: For addresses that appear to be IPv4 mapped addresses (starting with ::FFFF/96), this property will report the embedded IPv4 address. For any other address, this property will be None.

info.excerpt: For addresses that appear to be IPv4 mapped addresses (starting with ::FFFF/96), this property will report the embedded IPv4 address. For any other address, this property will be None.

description.en: IPv4Address object representing the IPv4-mapped IPv6 address. See RFC 4291. Added in version 3.13.

info.excerpt: IPv4Address object representing the IPv4-mapped IPv6 address. See RFC 4291. Added in version 3.13.

description.en: True if the address is reserved for link-local usage. See RFC 3927.

info.excerpt: True if the address is reserved for link-local usage. See RFC 3927.

description.en: These attributes are true for the network as a whole if they are true for both the network address and the broadcast address.

info.excerpt: These attributes are true for the network as a whole if they are true for both the network address and the broadcast address.

description.en: True if this is a loopback address. See RFC 3330 (for IPv4) or RFC 2373 (for IPv6).

info.excerpt: True if the address is reserved for multicast use. See RFC 3171 (for IPv4) or RFC 2373 (for IPv6).

description.en: True if the address is noted as reserved by the IETF. For IPv4, this is only 240.0.0.0/4, the Reserved address block. For IPv6, this is all addresses allocated as Reserved by IETF for future use. Note For IPv4, is_reserved is not related to the address block value of the Reserved-by-Protocol column in iana-ipv4-special-registry. Caution For IPv6, fec0::/10 a former Site-Local scoped address prefix is currently excluded from that list (see is_site_local & RFC 3879).

info.excerpt: True if the address is noted as reserved by the IETF. For IPv4, this is only 240.0.0.0/4, the Reserved address block. For IPv6, this is all addresses allocated as Reserved by IETF for future use. Note...

description.en: True if the address is reserved for site-local usage. Note that the site-local address space has been deprecated by RFC 3879. Use is_private to test if this address is in the space of unique local addresses as defined by RFC 4193.

info.excerpt: True if the address is reserved for site-local usage. Note that the site-local address space has been deprecated by RFC 3879. Use is_private to test if this address is in the space of unique local add...

description.en: This attribute is true for the network as a whole if it is true for both the network address and the broadcast address.

info.excerpt: This attribute is true for the network as a whole if it is true for both the network address and the broadcast address.

description.en: True if the address is unspecified. See RFC 5735 (for IPv4) or RFC 2373 (for IPv6).

info.excerpt: True if the address is unspecified. See RFC 5735 (for IPv4) or RFC 2373 (for IPv6).

description.en: Construct an IPv4 address. An AddressValueError is raised if address is not a valid IPv4 address. The following constitutes a valid IPv4 address: A string in decimal-dot notation, consisting of four decimal integers in the inclusive range 0–255, separated by dots (e.g. 192.168.0.1). Each integer represents an octet (byte) in the address. Leading zeroes are not tolerated to prevent confusion with octal notation. An integer that fits into 32 bits. An integer packed into a bytes object of length...

description.en: Construct an IPv4 interface. The meaning of address is as in the constructor of IPv4Network, except that arbitrary host addresses are always accepted. IPv4Interface is a subclass of IPv4Address, so it inherits all the attributes from that class. In addition, the following attributes are available: ip¶ The address (IPv4Address) without network information. >>> interface = IPv4Interface('192.0.2.5/24') >>> interface.ip IPv4Address('192.0.2.5') network¶ The network (IPv4Network) this interface b...

description.en: Construct an IPv4 network definition. address can be one of the following: A string consisting of an IP address and an optional mask, separated by a slash (/). The IP address is the network address, and the mask can be either a single number, which means it’s a prefix, or a string representation of an IPv4 address. If it’s the latter, the mask is interpreted as a net mask if it starts with a non-zero field, or as a host mask if it starts with a zero field, with the single exception of an all-...

description.en: Construct an IPv6 address. An AddressValueError is raised if address is not a valid IPv6 address. The following constitutes a valid IPv6 address: A string consisting of eight groups of four hexadecimal digits, each group representing 16 bits. The groups are separated by colons. This describes an exploded (longhand) notation. The string can also be compressed (shorthand notation) by various means. See RFC 4291 for details. For example, "0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0abc:0007:0def" can be compresse...

info.excerpt: Construct an IPv6 address. An AddressValueError is raised if address is not a valid IPv6 address. The following constitutes a valid IPv6 address: A string consisting of eight groups of four hexadecima...

description.en: Construct an IPv6 interface. The meaning of address is as in the constructor of IPv6Network, except that arbitrary host addresses are always accepted. IPv6Interface is a subclass of IPv6Address, so it inherits all the attributes from that class. In addition, the following attributes are available: ip¶ network¶ with_prefixlen¶ with_netmask¶ with_hostmask¶ Refer to the corresponding attribute documentation in IPv4Interface.

description.en: Construct an IPv6 network definition. address can be one of the following: A string consisting of an IP address and an optional prefix length, separated by a slash (/). The IP address is the network address, and the prefix length must be a single number, the prefix. If no prefix length is provided, it’s considered to be /128. Note that currently expanded netmasks are not supported. That means 2001:db00::0/24 is a valid argument while 2001:db00::0/ffff:ff00:: is not. An integer that fits into ...

description.en: Return an IPv4Address or IPv6Address object depending on the IP address passed as argument. Either IPv4 or IPv6 addresses may be supplied; integers less than 2**32 will be considered to be IPv4 by default. A ValueError is raised if address does not represent a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address. >>> ipaddress.ip_address('192.168.0.1') IPv4Address('192.168.0.1') >>> ipaddress.ip_address('2001:db8::') IPv6Address('2001:db8::')

description.en: Return an IPv4Interface or IPv6Interface object depending on the IP address passed as argument. address is a string or integer representing the IP address. Either IPv4 or IPv6 addresses may be supplied; integers less than 2**32 will be considered to be IPv4 by default. A ValueError is raised if address does not represent a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address.

description.en: Return an IPv4Network or IPv6Network object depending on the IP address passed as argument. address is a string or integer representing the IP network. Either IPv4 or IPv6 networks may be supplied; integers less than 2**32 will be considered to be IPv4 by default. strict is passed to IPv4Network or IPv6Network constructor. A ValueError is raised if address does not represent a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address, or if the network has host bits set. >>> ipaddress.ip_network('192.168.0.0/28') IPv4Netwo...
